evidence.scientific_clarity: Forest plot clearly conveys 12 per-study log-OR estimates with 95% CI lines, size-weighted squares, null-effect dashed line, and red inverse-variance pooled diamond; all elements readable.
evidence.typography: Script uses default DejaVu Sans (Matplotlib default) throughout; no pdf.fonttype=42 set, so PDF embeds Type3 fonts — text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for study points and red (#d62728) for pooled diamond are visually distinct; red is used as a special 'pooled' marker (not directional), but red+blue is not fully colorblind-safe (deuteranopia issues possible) and default palette was not explicitly chosen for accessibility.
evidence.layout_density: Panel space is well-used: y-range fits all 12 studies + pooled row with separator, annotations on the right side are accommodated by wider x-limit, no overlapping labels, no excessive whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption faithfully describes squares, CI lines, diamond, null line, and pooled estimate with exact numbers; legend present in lower-right; figure title matches task; no obvious mismatch between figure and caption.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (PDF 1.4, 28 KB, real vector structure), but fonts are embedded as Type3 (DejaVu Sans /Subtype /Type3) because pdf.fonttype=42 was never set — text is not selectable.
evidence.file_format: All four required files are present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (28 KB), figure.png (82 KB), caption.tex (557 B), gen_figure.py (3.1 KB). Additionally figure.svg is present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all visual encodings (squares, CI lines, diamond, null line), states the specific pooled estimate with CI and SE, and clarifies no imputation was done — a strong, informative caption.

overall_strengths: Complete, well-structured forest plot with all required visual elements (CI lines, size-weighted squares, pooled diamond, null-effect line, per-study annotations).
Caption is highly informative with exact numerical findings (pooled estimate, 95% CI, SE) and methodological note about data imputation.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set in the script, causing Type3 (non-selectable) font embedding in the PDF — text cannot be edited or searched in the vector output.
Uses Matplotlib default DejaVu Sans font family without explicit colorblind-safe palette selection; red+blue combination may pose issues for some colorblind readers.
